title: Local connection properties not saved when user has read only access to database
author: sparerd
body_snippet:
From #1348. Local connection properties (such as folder expansion states) are not saved when the user does not have write access to the database. We should be able to detect and handle this situation and still save local-only properties, even though database values won't be saved.

## Expected Behavior
Local connection properties should still be saved.

## Current Behavior
Local connection properties are not saved.

## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)

notes:
AI triage (claude-retry): Bug filed by maintainer (sparerd) — local connection properties (folder expansion) not saved when user has read-only DB access. Should detect and handle gracefully.
Needs human intervention after 12 AI attempts
Failed 5+ automated attempts (Sonnet). Requires manual fix or Opus-level agent.
[2026-03-01] Verified: SqlConnectionsSaver.Save() calls UpdateLocalConnectionProperties() at line 52 BEFORE the SQL ReadOnly check. Local properties (Expanded, Connected, Favorite) are always saved to LocalConnectionProperties.xml regardless of DB access.
